title: The Cries of the Trees

"'''The Cries of the Trees'''" is the pilot episode and series premiere of the American animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], which premiered on [[ABC Television Network|ABC]] on [[September 7]], [[1985]]. It was written by [[Paul Dini]] and directed by [[Raymond Jafelice]]. In [[1997]], "The Cries of the Trees" was compiled in the direct-to-video feature [[The Haunted Village (film)|''The Haunted Village'']].

Set in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the episode follows [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] ([[Jim Henshaw]]) and [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]] ([[Cree Summer]]) as they defend the [[Soul Tree]]s from the evil [[Tulgah/Legends|Tulgah]] [[Witch/Legends|witch]], [[Morag]].

==Official description==
''Wicket and his pals defend the Soul Trees when Morag tries to set the forest on fire.''

==Plot summary==
===Fire danger===
It is a time of drought on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] friends meet [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Izrina]] of the [[Wistie/Legends|Wisties]], but are told off by [[Bozzie]] and return to their village. Suddenly the Wisties are attacked by a band of [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzums]] who serve [[Morag]], the [[Tulgah/Legends|Tulgah]] [[Witch/Legends|witch]]. Izrina is captured by Morag personally.

Back in [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], the young Ewoks do their chores. [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]] approaches them and suggests that they play [[drop-the-sack]] instead. Meanwhile, in [[Morag's lair]], the witch casts a spell on Izrina, turning the queen into an evil, [[Fire/Legends|flaming]] creature. Morag releases Izrina to infect the other Wisties and burn down the Ewoks' [[Soul Tree]]s.

Paploo, Wicket, and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] play the forbidden game of [[drop-the-sack]] in the [[Happy Grove/Legends|Happy Grove]]. They accidentally drop a sack of mud on the matronly [[Bozzie]], and the Elders see through Paploo's hasty lie to protect himself and his friends. As such, the young Ewoks are assigned cleaning duty on the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] floor. As they work, Izrina flies by and sets the area ablaze. [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] and some other elders arrive and put out the fire. The young Ewoks are held responsible, disbelieving their claim that the fire seemed to begin on its own since the young ones had lied before, and are sentenced to a three-[[Standard day/Legends|day]] detention in a remote area under the guardianship of Wicket's older brothers.

===Saving the forests===
That night, the three young Ewoks share the same [[Dream/Legends|dream]]: The Soul [[Tree/Legends|Trees]] are burning and crying out to them for help. The Ewoks rush outside to discover that the forest is ablaze. When they alert their warders, the cursed Izrina attacks. Wicket douses the Wistie in [[Water/Legends|water]], removing the curse. Izrina tells them about Morag's scheme, and all the Ewoks hurry to warn Chirpa.

They interrupt the [[Festival of Hoods]], but no one believes them. However, while Wicket's brothers and Izrina explain the situation to [[Shaman/Legends|Shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], the youngest Ewoks, including [[Malani]], sense the distress of the trees. Chirpa is convinced and mobilizes the community to fight the fire, but Morag interferes by damming the river to cut off their water supply. Logray and Wicket retrieve [[magic foam]], which proves to be useful against the blaze and the cursed Wisties. However, Morag interferes again and damages the [[Catapult/Legends|catapults]] necessary to launch the bags against the fire.

Guided by the Wisties, Wicket and [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]] fly [[Hang-glider/Legends|hang gliders]] above the trees to airdrop more bags of foam, saving the Soul Trees. Observing the young ones' bravery and ingenuity in this crisis, Chirpa and Logray decide that they were too hard on them, although Bozzie needs to be convinced when Wicket observes her making a last stand against the fire protecting her personal Soul Tree, and he does a precise airdrop to save both. It begins to rain, and Bozzie reconciles with Wicket.
